pr.title: Match some functions in unsplit it files
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Matched and improved several item-system functions in the unsplit it_266F and it_2725 translation units. The decomp.dev report credited the PR with +3776 matched bytes, 15 newly matched functions, and 4 improved unmatched functions, primarily by tightening types, adjusting local variable lifetimes/order, using item access macros, adding stack padding, and applying dont_inline pragmas around small wrapper/cmd functions.

key_files.role: largest matching work; item throw/velocity helpers, FtCmd item command wrappers, Game & Watch helper, const data, scoped locals, dont_inline pragmas
matched/improved lower item helpers including collision update, landing/throw counter logic, mpLib query, dynamic bone loops
updated fn_80279B30 and it_8027CE64 prototypes to match implementation/signature discoveries
corrected Item and ItemCommonData field types used by matched item code
changed Game & Watch Panic attr local type to align with it_8027CE64 void pointer usage

what_changed: decomp.dev reported matched code rose to 28.65%, a +0.10% gain totaling +3776 bytes.

15 functions reached 100% according to the bot report: it_80273748, it_80273B50, it_8027CE64, it_8027A09C, it_8026D8A4, it_802798D4, it_80279AF0, it_8026EBC8, it_8026EC54, fn_80279B30, it_8026E9A4, it_8026DC24, it_8026DDFC, it_8026DE98, and it_80272980.

4 unmatched functions improved: it_802775F0 to 97.18%, it_80279888 to 84.21%, it_8026C47C to 97.78%, and it_8027737C to 96.49%. The bot also noted a small percentage regression for unmatched it_8026E15C.
Replaced several direct user_data/hsd_obj accesses with GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ macros where that produced better matches.
Adjusted local variable declaration order and scope in larger throw/update helpers, especially it_80273748 and it_80273B50, to influence register allocation and lifetimes.
Added explicit padding/local dummy arrays such as u8 _[4], PAD_STACK(4), stack_top[9], and u32 pad[1] where stack layout mattered.
Changed it_804DC76C and it_804DC7F8 to static const values, affecting data placement/constant handling.

Changed Item.xD54_throwNum from s32 to u32 and ItemCommonData.x48_float to u8 x48_byte; it_8026DDFC now reads the x48 byte through a u8 pointer before extracting nibbles for throw/land destruction logic.

Updated fn_80279B30 to accept a third int argument, compute idx = arg2 - 21, and dispatch through it_804D51C8[idx](item_gobj, cmd) instead of the previous placeholder varargs/negative-index expression.
Updated it_8027CE64 and its header prototype to take void* arg_attr_address, then store it directly in item->xDD4_itemVar.it_27CE.attr; itgamewatchpanic.c changed its attr local from int* to void**.
Wrapped selected small functions with #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop, including it_80279888/it_802798D4, it_80279AF0, it_8027A09C, and it_8027CE64.
smart_moves: Used inner blocks to deliberately narrow Item*, CollData*, and HSD_JObj* lifetimes, which is a practical MWCC matching tactic for register pressure in large unsplit files.
Replaced broad or misleading field types with the smallest evidenced type: x48_byte as u8 enabled byte/nibble access instead of treating the field as a word-sized float-like value.
Turned a guessed function-pointer table call in fn_80279B30 into a clean indexed callback using the recovered third argument, improving both readability and match quality.
Matched Game & Watch Panic attribute passing by making it_8027CE64 accept a void* and storing the pointer directly instead of taking the address of the local argument.
Kept vector component assignments explicit rather than struct assignment in it_80273500, consistent with the diff note that direct assignment can become memcpy rather than float transfers.
Used dont_inline pragmas surgically around wrappers/cmd helpers rather than globally changing optimization behavior.
Simplified boolean float-zero comparisons from == 0.0f to !field in several spots where that matched codegen.
decomp_lessons: In unsplit item files, matching may depend as much on local scope, declaration order, and temporary lifetime as on high-level logic.
Prefer GET_ITEM and GET_JOBJ when the expected codegen matches project access macros, but direct user_data may still be needed in tightly scoped regions if it affects allocation.
When a common-data field is used only for bit/nibble extraction, verify whether it is actually a byte field; using a word-sized placeholder can force wrong loads and shifts.
Function-pointer dispatcher stubs should recover all hidden/original parameters before resorting to varargs or M2C_ERROR-style placeholders.
For small wrapper functions, inlining control can be the deciding factor for matching adjacent command handlers.
Header prototypes and call sites should be updated together when a recovered signature changes; otherwise the implementation match may leave type debt elsewhere.
Const qualification on file-static float/double constants can matter for matching rodata/sdata placement in these item translation units.
A small percentage regression in one unmatched function may accompany broader matching gains; record it rather than assuming every touched function improved.
naming_conventions: Preserved address-style names for unknown item functions: it_8026C47C, it_80273748, it_80273B50, it_8027CE64, fn_80279B30.
Used project item object aliases and macros such as Item_GObj, HSD_GObj, GET_ITEM, GET_JOBJ, and ItemKind/It_Kind_* where applicable.
Renamed ItemCommonData.x48_float to x48_byte after evidence showed byte/nibble usage.
Kept offset-based struct field names such as xD50_landNum, xD54_throwNum, x378_itemColl, xDD4_itemVar, and xDCF_flag while improving known type information.
Used descriptive temporary names in recovered code such as it_kind, hold_kind, jobj_parent, owner_gobj, item_jobj1, item_jobj2, and idx without inventing semantic names beyond evidence.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Scoped locals inside braces to control register allocation and reloads.
Adjusted local declaration order in large routines where comments in the removed code indicated register-allocation sensitivity.
Added or reduced PAD_STACK and dummy locals, including u8 _[4], u32 pad[1], and stack_top[9], to fit stack frame layout.
Changed unsigned suffixes and comparison forms, for example 1U to 1 and == 0.0f to !field, to match emitted instructions.
Moved HSD_JObj* jobj and jobj_parent declarations outside loops in it_8026EBC8 and it_8026EC54 to match loop register usage.
Used #pragma dont_inline on selected wrappers to prevent MWCC from inlining functions that must remain separate for matching.
Used byte pointer access to it_804D6D28->x48_byte before shift/mask operations, matching byte load semantics.
Replaced it_80272CC0(item_gobj, item->...bone_attach_id) with it_80272C90(item_gobj) where the helper encapsulated the same access pattern and matched codegen.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only issue comment was the decomp.dev bot report showing +3776 matched bytes, 15 new matches, 4 improvements, and 1 small unmatched-function regression.
searchable_terms: Match some functions in unsplit it files
follow_up_queries: Find other item functions using ItemCommonData x48_float/x48_byte or nibble extraction to confirm type consistency.
Search for remaining direct item_gobj->user_data accesses in it_2725.c that might match better as GET_ITEM or as scoped direct loads.
Inspect callers of fn_80279B30 to verify the recovered third argument meaning and whether arg2 should remain int or become an enum/command id later.
Search for other Game & Watch item helpers that pass attribute pointers to it_8027CE64 or store into xDD4_itemVar.it_27CE.attr.
Review remaining unmatched wrappers around it_80279888 and FtCmdState handlers for additional dont_inline or stack-padding opportunities.
Investigate the reported small regression in it_8026E15C if it blocks future matching.
